when: Structured analysis (risks, coupling, quality)
steps: Read blueprint.md and traverse the graph from entry-point.md.
Produce a structured analysis in docs/architecture/work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: analysis): Context, Findings, Recommendations, Traceability.
Link to existing guardrail findings in blueprint.md if relevant.
Register in blueprint.md (WRK-NNN). Verify links.
placeholders: e.g. coupling, failure modes, security, performance

when: Resume open WRK items in blueprint.md
steps: Read docs/architecture/blueprint.md ## Work register (or ## Architecture work).
Pick the next item with Track: architecture (or untagged legacy row) and status draft, or start a new query/analysis/design if requested.
Update the work file and blueprint before stopping.

when: Architecture design proposal
steps: Read blueprint.md and relevant work/ items and <template> sections.
Write design to docs/architecture/work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: design): Context, Design (with Mermaid), Alternatives, Impact, Traceability.
If the design implies a decision, draft ADR in <template>/decisions/ and cross-link.
Register in blueprint.md (WRK-NNN). Verify links.
placeholders: e.g. add circuit breaker between order-service and payment-service
optional: latency, no new infra, etc.

when: Dialog to explore a solution (one question per reply)
steps: Goal / question: <your question here>
PHASE 1 — INTERVIEW (active until explicit end)
This workflow is a **dialog to explore the solution step by step**.
It OVERRIDES role-architecture-work.md (steps 3–5 and OUTPUT_CONTRACT) until Phase 2 begins.
- Do not create, modify, or propose creating files
- No Mermaid diagrams, architecture sketches, or design proposals
- No recommendation lists, trade-offs, or solution drafts
- No [[ANCHOR:...]] output
- No more than ONE question per reply
- In the FIRST reply: short interview plan (3–5 bullets) + exactly question 1
- In follow-up replies: optionally max. 2 sentences of context/progress + exactly ONE question
- Adjust the plan when the human revises earlier answers
**Progress:** Point X of Y — [topic]
**Brief:** [optional, max. 2 sentences]
**Question:** [exactly one question]
PHASE 2 — WRITE (only after explicit signal)
- the human writes "end interview", "done", or "write it up", OR
- all plan items are clarified and the human agrees.

Then apply role-architecture-work.md, write file under <doc-root>work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: design), WRK entry in <doc-root>blueprint.md, output [[ANCHOR:WORK_ITEM]], [[ANCHOR:TRACEABILITY_COVERAGE]], [[ANCHOR:OPEN_QUESTIONS]], [[ANCHOR:LINK_CHECK]].
1. Read <doc-root>blueprint.md and traverse from <doc-root>entry-point.md (silently in the background — do not dump as a wall of text).
2. First reply: brief understanding (1 sentence) + interview plan (3–5 bullets) + question 1.
3. Each further reply: progress + optional brief + exactly one question. Always wait for the human.
4. On revision: adjust plan, re-clarify affected points.
In your FIRST reply, confirm dialog mode, show the plan, and ask question 1. Do not invent a solution upfront.

when: Answer a specific architecture question using the graph
steps: Read docs/architecture/blueprint.md, entry-point.md, and prompts/role-architecture-work.md.
Traverse the Markdown link graph only; follow imports/exports, <template>/, and ops links.
Do not scan raw source unless a link leads there.
Write the answer to docs/architecture/work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md using work/_template.md (type: question).
Register the item in blueprint.md ## Work register (next WRK-NNN, Track: architecture).
Verify all links resolve.

when: Sustainable software analysis — structure, drift, technical debt
prerequisite: Bootstrap phase 0 `[x] done`; `entry-point.md` and source map in `always-on.md`
steps: Read <doc-root>blueprint.md, traverse from <doc-root>entry-point.md, and read <doc-root>context/always-on.md (source map).
Inspect source under Scope and Source paths — follow links from the graph first; extend to code only where evidence is needed.
If Compare to documented architecture is yes: contrast intended structure (<template> sections, building blocks, interfaces) with observed code structure; record drift explicitly.

Produce docs/architecture/work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: analysis) using this structure: - Context & scope - Intended vs actual architecture (drift) — skip subsection if compare=no and no docs exist - Structural quality (modularity, boundaries, package/module layout) - Layering & dependencies (violations, cycles, wrong-direction deps) - Coupling & cohesion (hotspots, god modules, inappropriate cross-cutting) - Technical debt (categorized findings with severity: critical / major / minor) - Domain & naming (ubiquitous language, bounded contexts if applicable) - Maintainability & changeability (extension points, test seams, change cost) - Recommendations (prioritized, sized: quick win / medium / strategic) - Traceability (every major claim → doc link or source path)
Limit analysis to Focus dimensions when not `<unspecified>`; mention omitted dimensions briefly in Context.
Link to existing guardrail findings in blueprint.md; add new guardrail candidates in Recommendations.
Register in <doc-root>blueprint.md (WRK-NNN). Verify relative links.
placeholders: modules, services, packages, or repository paths

when: Dialog — define scope and focus, then sustainable software analysis
steps: Method: sustainable software architecture
Initial goal (optional): <initial-goal>
PHASE 1 — INTERVIEW (active until explicit end)
This workflow is a **dialog to scope and focus a sustainable architecture analysis** before writing.
It OVERRIDES role-architecture-work.md (steps 3–5 and OUTPUT_CONTRACT) until Phase 2 begins.
- Analysis scope (modules, services, packages, or paths)
- Focus dimensions (architecture drift, modularity, layering, coupling, technical debt, domain model, maintainability)
- Whether to compare documented vs actual architecture
- Optional source paths beyond always-on.md source map
- Work file slug for the final report
- Do not create, modify, or propose creating files
- No analysis findings, recommendations, or Mermaid diagrams
- No [[ANCHOR:...]] output
- No more than ONE question per reply
- First reply: short interview plan (3–5 bullets) + exactly question 1
- Follow-ups: max. 2 sentences context + exactly ONE question
- Adjust plan when the human revises earlier answers
**Progress:** Point X of Y — [topic]
**Brief:** [optional, max. 2 sentences]
**Question:** [exactly one question]
PHASE 2 — ANALYSIS (write)
- the human writes "end interview", "done", or "write it up", OR
- scope, focus, compare flag, and slug are all agreed.
Then apply role-architecture-work.md and write <doc-root>work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: analysis).
1. Read <doc-root>blueprint.md, traverse from <doc-root>entry-point.md, read <doc-root>context/always-on.md.
2. Inspect source under agreed scope; follow graph links first.
3. Write analysis with sections: Context & scope | Intended vs actual (drift) | Structural quality |
Layering & dependencies | Coupling & cohesion | Technical debt | Domain & naming |
Maintainability & changeability | Recommendations | Traceability.
4. Register WRK-NNN in <doc-root>blueprint.md. Link guardrail findings where relevant.
In your FIRST reply, confirm dialog mode, show the plan, and ask question 1. Do not start the analysis upfront.

when: First-time adoption; no `docs/architecture/blueprint.md` yet
steps: If <doc-root>/blueprint.md already exists, stop and tell the human to paste the bootstrap-continue session prompt in a new chat instead.

Verify Phase A in prompts/reference/adopt-procedure.md: role-bootstrap.md and prompts/core/system-prompt.md must exist. If missing, stop — ask the human to run `agm scaffold` or the install script (no git clone).
If an **Adoption parameters** block is present (with **File roles**), create always-on.md, blueprint.md, and entry-point.md as separate files under <doc-root>; interview only for missing facts.
If **Architecture documentation areas (bootstrap)** is present, add blueprint phases and doc stubs for each selected area per prompts/reference/doc-extensions.md (your Markdown only).
Bootstrap: follow <doc-root>/prompts/role-bootstrap.md — construction plan in blueprint.md, navigation in entry-point.md, first evidence-based template section.
Verify relative links. Append a session log entry to blueprint.md.

when: Resume blueprint phases after Bootstrap phase 0 is `[x] done`
steps: Follow the AGM core prompt and docs/architecture/prompts/role-bootstrap.md.
Read docs/architecture/context/always-on.md → docs/architecture/blueprint.md.
Resume the next [~] or [ ] phase.

when: First-time setup; no `docs/architecture/blueprint.md` yet
steps: Bootstrap AGM documentation for this application.
Follow docs/architecture/prompts/role-bootstrap.md (copy from docs/templates/architecture/prompts/ if missing).
Read prompts/core/system-prompt.md rules. Create docs/architecture/ structure from the chosen template.

when: Incorporate pasted external material (Confluence, Markdown, specs, use cases, internal notes) into the architecture graph
steps: Read <doc-root>context/always-on.md → <doc-root>blueprint.md → <doc-root>prompts/role-bootstrap.md → prompts/reference/content-ingest.md.
Redact secrets, credentials, and PII from pasted content before writing files; ask the human if confidentiality is unclear.

Create <doc-root>sources/ if missing (index.md, log.md). Persist the import in <doc-root>sources/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: source-ingest) with provenance frontmatter; update sources/index.md and sources/log.md.

Extract structured facts into scoped architecture sections — e.g. use-cases/, <template>/introduction.md, context.md, glossary, interfaces/imports.md, constraints, context/on-demand.md, domain/ when installed. Link every extracted claim back to the source-ingest file.
When paste describes scenarios, create or update <doc-root>use-cases/<slug>.md (type: use-case) with source link.
Cross-check code via always-on.md source map when claims imply implementation; record conflicts as open assumptions.
Update entry-point.md links and blueprint.md session log; adjust relevant blueprint phase rows when new areas are populated.
If **Architecture documentation areas (evolve)** is present, prioritize those areas when placing extracted content.

when: Identify bounded contexts and strategic relationships
steps: Read blueprint.md, domain/, interfaces/, entry-point.md; traverse graph before scanning source.
Identify bounded contexts (business capability + ubiquitous language boundary).
Update <doc-root>domain/context-map.md (contexts table + Mermaid map + integration patterns).
Map each context to implementation paths and interfaces/ contracts.
Write <doc-root>work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: domain-analysis) with findings and recommendations.
Record matching ddd-guardrails.md IDs in recommendations; propose blueprint guardrail rows.
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.
placeholders: optional: greenfield | legacy extraction | integration cleanup
systems, modules, services, or repository paths

when: Resume open domain WRK items
steps: Read <doc-root>blueprint.md ## Work register.
Pick the next item with Track: domain and status draft, or continue the domain task the human names.
Update domain/ graph files and work item before stopping.

when: Domain model or boundary design (aggregates, ACL, context split)
prerequisite: Bootstrap phase 0 `[x] done`; context map recommended for cross-context designs
steps: Read domain/, relevant work/ items, interfaces/, template decisions/.
Write design to <doc-root>work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: domain-design): model, aggregates, events, integration impact.
Draft updates to domain/contexts/<context>/model.md (link from work item).
Strategic boundary change → draft ADR in <template>/decisions/ and update context-map.md.
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.
placeholders: e.g. split Order aggregate, introduce ACL to payment context, model refund policy

when: Domain discovery via Event Storming (dialog)
steps: Process / bounded context (optional): <process-or-context>
Initial goal (optional): <initial-goal>
Report format: prompts/reference/ddd-work-report-formats.md § Event storm
PHASE 1 — EVENT STORM INTERVIEW
Dialog to build a shared domain timeline. OVERRIDES role-domain-work.md (steps 3–7 and OUTPUT_CONTRACT) until Phase 2.
- Do not create or modify files
- No aggregate diagrams, context maps, or design proposals
- No [[ANCHOR:...]] output
- No more than ONE question per reply
- First reply: storm plan (domain events → commands → aggregates → policies) + question 1
- Follow-ups: progress + one question about the next event, command, policy, or hotspot
- Use orange/purple/pink/yellow stickies metaphor in prose (events, commands, aggregates, policies)
1) Domain events in chronological order (past tense: "OrderPlaced")
2) Commands that trigger events
3) Aggregate candidates that accept commands
4) Policies / reactions (when X then Y)
5) Read models and external systems
6) Hot spots (uncertainty, contention, legacy)
**Progress:** Step X of Y — [topic]
**Brief:** [optional, max. 2 sentences]
**Question:** [exactly one question]
PHASE 2 — WRITE
Start when human writes "end interview", "done", or "write it up".
1. Read <doc-root>blueprint.md, domain/, entry-point.md (background only).
2. Write <doc-root>work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: domain-discovery) per report format.
3. Add candidate events to <doc-root>domain/events.md (draft rows) if agreed.
4. Optionally create <doc-root>domain/contexts/<slug>/model.md draft if one context emerged.
5. Register WRK in blueprint (Track: domain). Verify links.

when: Project an E2 board snapshot (board-snapshot-v2) into the AGM domain graph
steps: Read always-on → blueprint → role-domain-work → e2-board-projection → content-ingest.
Validate snapshot (format event-storming-tool, version 2); redact secrets.
Persist sources/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.storm.json + source-ingest note (sourceType: e2-board-snapshot).
Build Projection Manifest (Intent/Tier/Graph/Maturity filters); stop for human gate unless pre-approved.
Apply approved rows only; write work report; optional code cross-check (code wins).
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.

when: Review cross-context integration against DDD patterns
steps: Read domain/context-map.md, interfaces/exports.md, interfaces/imports.md, ecosystem-index if present.
For each integration, classify pattern: ACL, OHS, conformist, partnership, shared kernel, separate ways.
Contrast documented context map relationships with actual contracts.
Flag ddd-guardrails.md strategic smells (DDD-S01–S06).
Update context-map.md relationship table if corrections are evidence-based.
Write work report (type: domain-analysis) with prioritized recommendations.
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.
placeholders: cross-service integrations, API surface, or named context pair

when: Ubiquitous language audit (docs vs code vs APIs)
steps: Collect term inventory from domain language docs and template glossary.
Collect names from Scope source (types, modules, REST/event names) — follow graph links first.
Build mismatch matrix: synonym clusters, deprecated terms, technical leaks.
Recommend one canonical term per concept per context.
Apply agreed renames to language.md / glossary / on-demand.md only where human confirms in session; otherwise list in report.
Write work report (type: domain-analysis); flag DDD-T05, DDD-T06.
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.
placeholders: bounded context, service, or module paths

when: Answer a domain / DDD question using the graph
steps: Read blueprint.md, domain/, entry-point.md, prompts/role-domain-work.md.
Traverse Markdown graph; prefer domain/, glossary, interfaces/, contexts/ over raw source.
Write answer to <doc-root>work/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md (type: domain-question) using _template-domain.md.
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.

when: Classify subdomains (core / supporting / generic) for investment
steps: Read domain/, blueprint.md, entry-point.md; link to business goals in template docs.
Inventory subdomains (finer-grained than bounded contexts where helpful).
Classify each: core | supporting | generic with explicit rationale.
Update <doc-root>domain/subdomains.md.
Write work report (type: domain-analysis) with investment implications.
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.
placeholders: product line, platform, or enterprise area

when: Tactical DDD review (aggregates, invariants, events, repositories)
steps: Read domain/contexts/<context>/model.md and language.md if present; traverse from entry-point.md.
Inspect source under Scope for aggregate roots, entities, VOs, repositories, domain services, events.
Check: consistency boundaries, anemic model, repository scope, domain vs integration events.
Map findings to ddd-guardrails.md tactical IDs (DDD-T01–T08).
Propose updates to model.md and events.md where evidence supports.
Write work report (type: domain-analysis).
Register WRK (Track: domain). Verify links.

when: After code changes; agent fetches `git diff` between two refs (CI-friendly)
steps: Read <doc-root>context/always-on.md → <doc-root>blueprint.md → <doc-root>prompts/role-maintenance.md.

Obtain the code diff between DIFF_FROM and DIFF_TO yourself — do not ask the human to paste it: a) If a Git MCP server is connected, use its diff/compare tool with the same refs. b) Otherwise run at repository root: git diff ${DIFF_FROM}..${DIFF_TO} c) If the range is a PR merge base, use three-dot when appropriate: git diff ${DIFF_FROM}...${DIFF_TO}
If the diff is empty, stop and report; do not update architecture docs.
Classify changes and update only architecture docs impacted by this diff.
If **Architecture documentation areas (evolve)** is present, update those architecture Markdown files when the diff affects them.

when: After code changes; same PR or follow-up session
steps: Read docs/architecture/context/always-on.md → docs/architecture/blueprint.md → docs/architecture/prompts/role-maintenance.md.
<paste git diff or PR diff summary>
Update only architecture docs impacted by this diff.
If **Architecture documentation areas (evolve)** is present, update those architecture Markdown files when the diff affects them.
placeholders: paste git diff or PR diff summary

when: Targeted deepening of specific documentation sections (ongoing on large systems)
steps: Goal: <goal>
Scope: <scope> (from documentation focus checkboxes or optional text — architecture content only; never entry-point.md, blueprint.md, or always-on.md)
Follow <doc-root>prompts/role-bootstrap.md for evidence and link rules.
Read always-on.md → blueprint.md. Update only the scoped sections and blueprint states.
If **Architecture documentation areas (evolve)** is present, update those Markdown areas within the stated scope (not prompts/ or workflows).
Do not restart unrelated phases.

when: After a maintenance run
steps: Review AGM documentation for this application.
Review type: maintenance
Cross-check only files changed in the last maintenance run against the git diff.
Do not fix issues — report only.

when: After Bootstrap milestone — close creation
steps: Review AGM documentation for this application.
Review type: milestone (bootstrap close)
1. Validate docs/architecture/blueprint.md — phase states, WRK registry, session log, and open items match the actual documentation graph.
2. Cross-check the full docs/architecture/ graph for consistency, stale content, and broken relative links.

3. From bootstrap follow-up sessions: find insights still only in work/ files or the session log that belong in template sections (arc42, c4-light, etc.). Recommend where to link or summarize them — report-only; do not rewrite architecture docs in this session.
Report-only. Write report to docs/architecture/work/. Update blueprint.md ## Reviews.

when: After each blueprint phase; verify one phase
steps: Read docs/architecture/context/always-on.md → docs/architecture/blueprint.md → docs/architecture/prompts/role-review.md.
Select next unreviewed phase in ## Reviews. Report-only — do not fix files.
Write work/YYYY-MM-DD-review-<slug>.md. Update ## Reviews and ## Guardrail findings in blueprint.md.
